entries.question: The defending block moves toward the ball side.
subjectiveClaims: Does not prove attacking intent or that a switch should have been played.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Keep as the approved M1 family; document signed_lateral_shift as a high-level detector stage rather than a pure primitive.

entries.question: A progressive passing corridor opens and later closes.
subjectiveClaims: Current implementation supports geometric corridor, not pass probability or receiver decision quality.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Use existing relation episodes and expose open_frame_id, open_confirm_frame_id, close_frame_id, duration_seconds, minimum_clearance_m, and limiting_defender_id as evidence.

entries.question: A controlled line break occurs.
subjectiveClaims: Controlled must be defined as measurable possession/ball-active state, not subjective control quality.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Add defensive-line-relative position first; derive line-break episodes from a player or ball crossing from in-front to beyond the line.

entries.question: Support arrives after a line break.
subjectiveClaims: Support must be defined by distance, lane, or progressive option criteria.
entries.recommendedNextStep: After line-break anchors exist, implement support_arrival_relation as an anchor-relative relation with arrival delay, support count, and lane diversity evidence.

entries.question: A wide ball carrier becomes isolated.
subjectiveClaims: Isolation should be measured by support distance/count and pressure, not asserted as decision quality.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Define wide isolation as wide ball location plus low progressive/inside support count and optionally rising nearest-defender pressure.

entries.question: A local attacking overload forms.
subjectiveClaims: Overload should be thresholded as an attacker-minus-defender count, not a qualitative shape judgment.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Add an anchor-relative local numerical difference primitive that returns attacker count, defender count, and delta for a region/radius.

entries.question: A pressing trap develops near the touchline.
subjectiveClaims: Trap is causal/tactical language; deterministic query should lower to touchline proximity, pressure increase, and reduced exits.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Do not add a trap detector first; add measurable pressure and local-number components, then compose a recipe.

entries.question: A runner attacks the space behind the last line.
subjectiveClaims: Attacks space should be represented as a progressive movement episode crossing or moving beyond a line.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Add defensive-line-relative position before forward-run episode; the run can lower to velocity/progression plus line-relative destination.

entries.question: Attackers occupy complementary vertical lanes.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Add lane occupancy as a general L2 capability; current lane fields only exist inside corridor relation evidence.

entries.question: Rest-defence numbers are insufficient during possession.
subjectiveClaims: Insufficient must be a declared numeric threshold.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Implement local/rest-defence numerical counts with explicit thresholds and evidence, not a qualitative insufficient-shape primitive.

entries.question: Support arrives after an attacking regain.
subjectiveClaims: Attacking regain should be measured from possession/team-role transition, not inferred intent.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Add regain anchors from possession transition, then reuse support_arrival_relation.

entries.question: A passing option appears, persists, and disappears unused.
subjectiveClaims: Unused can currently mean ball did not enter the destination region; it cannot prove a pass was available or intentionally declined.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Create an experimental composition anchored on possession/carry with corridor open/close evidence and destination-not-entered classification.

entries.question: A weak-side access route opens after lateral circulation.
subjectiveClaims: Weak-side access can be geometric; lateral circulation needs an explicit ball-movement definition.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Reuse opposite-side corridor logic after adding a lateral-circulation anchor derived from ball-side changes over time.

entries.question: A third-player / third-man supporting run develops.
dataLimitations: Canonical event data exists in the pipeline, but current runtime catalog does not expose pass/event anchors.
subjectiveClaims: Third-man is tactical language; deterministic version should be a third attacker entering a support lane/window after an anchor.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Implement support arrival and lane occupancy first; avoid claiming third-man intent.

entries.question: A counterpress produces or fails to produce local numerical superiority.
subjectiveClaims: Produces is causal wording; deterministic query can compare local numerical difference before/after loss in a fixed window.
entries.recommendedNextStep: Add local numerical difference and turnover/counterpress anchors before any counterpress recipe.

notes: Several questions contain subjective football wording, but none are subjective-only if rephrased into measurable thresholds.
No question requires pausing S2I or Workbench Alpha for an IR redesign; missing vocabulary can be added incrementally as primitives or relations.

visibilityTierLegend.USER_TACTICAL: Named concepts appropriate for direct user-facing recipes.
visibilityTierLegend.AGENT_COMPOSABLE: Safe building blocks Hermes may use in experimental plans after their contracts are complete.
visibilityTierLegend.COMPILER_LOWERING: Low-level geometry, kinematics, predicate, or source-fact lowering vocabulary not normally shown in recipe discovery.
visibilityTierLegend.TRUSTED_RECIPE_ONLY: High-level bundled logic usable only inside a reviewed recipe until hidden dependencies are formalized or split.
visibilityTierLegend.LEGACY_DOCUMENTATION: Historical or compatibility-only behavior, not a current authoring target.

standardLibraryExtensionChecklist: at least one real tactical consumer

hiddenDependencyTreatments.note: Formalize all shared runtime parameters in the catalog contract before broad direct Hermes exposure; existing reviewed recipes may keep supplying them at recipe level.
Keep restricted until its actual detector-stage inputs and selection policy are explicit, or split it into lower-level reusable capabilities.
Keep restricted because this is high-level M1 recipe logic, not a general primitive contract.
Make shared timing and internal geometric lowering dependencies explicit without exposing raw coordinate internals as user-facing recipe vocabulary.
Document this as an input-contract adapter for the same relation, not a separate football concept.

planningAcceptance: Accepted for architectural planning only; this matrix is not authorization to implement every missing primitive or relation.

deferredCapabilities.note: Valuable; may become part of the support-arrival relation or the next addition after the second-family package.
Wait until wide-isolation, pressing, and counterpress queries justify the exact metric.

restrictedCurrentCapabilities.reason: Hidden detector-stage dependencies are supplied by the reviewed M1 recipe.
Hidden M1 outcome dependencies are supplied by the reviewed M1 recipe.
